text: The video shows a clear view of the supravesical hernia, visible as a defect in the supravesical fossa.

The video shows the supravesical fossa, a triangular anatomical area bounded laterally and superiorly by the median and medial umbilical ligaments, and inferiorly by the peritoneal reflection extending from the anterior abdominal wall to the dome of the bladder.

The surgeon secures the mesh to Cooper's ligaments using ethibond sutures in a simple, interrupted fashion, ensuring the mesh remains in place over the hernia defect.
